entry.title: You are the Voice of HR! Participate Now in Sapient Insights Group’s 29th Annual HR Systems Survey
entry.content:
The 29th Annual HR Systems Survey, conducted by Sapient Insights Group, is now open for participation through June 25th, 2025.  Your voice is the future of HR Technology!

 

TAKE THE SURVEY

This survey is valuable for anyone supporting their organization's HR systems and practices, interested in sharing their vendor experiences, and giving back to the HR community.

All responses are confidential and only used in aggregate results.

In 2025, over 4,600 organizations across the globe participated!

As a Thank You for your participation, you will receive the following:

  • Continuing education – Participants can earn up to 11 HRIP PDUs
    (Professional Development Units) - 10 for the survey + 1 more for the IHRIM member-exclusive webinar on December 16, 2026
  • A personalized data snapshot filtered by Industry or Region – your pick!
  • A full copy of the 29th Annual Sapient Insights 2026-2027 HR Systems Survey Report (350+ pages), an $1800 value, to be released in the Fall of 2026.

 

The first 100 respondents who complete the minimum survey requirements will also receive a $10 gift card of their choice or option to make a charitable donation, and our 29th, 129th, 529th, 1029th, and 2029th respondents will be awarded a $100 gift card of their choice or option to make a charitable donation.

The 29th Edition of the Annual HR Systems Survey includes these exciting updates:

The first 100 respondents who complete the minimum survey requirements will also receive a $10 gift card of their choice or option to make a $10 charitable donation, and our 29th, 129th, 529th, 1029th, and 2029th respondents will be awarded a $100 gift card or option to make a $100 charitable donation.

The 29th Edition of the Annual HR Systems Survey includes:

  • Extensive questions on the use of AI / Machine Learning throughout the survey
  • More options to collaborate with colleagues to complete the survey
  • A dynamic table of contents that allows you to easily navigate through the survey, making the best use of your time and expertise
  • Easily stored previous answers, so there is no pressure to complete the survey in one sitting
  • A 24-hour response time to any technical issues or survey questions
  • A YouTube video on “How to take the survey”

Survey topics include:

  • HR strategy, process, and structure
  • HR technology adoption in all applications
  • HR practices in Benefits, Talent Management, and Workforce planning
  • HRIS governance & organization structure
  • Functional & HR Tech support services
  • Deployment roadmaps, resourcing, and budgeting
  • Implementation, maintenance, and integration of HR Tech platforms
  • Change management and adaptive leadership practices
  • Emerging and innovative technology and trends

New section this year on finance, travel expense reporting, and financial planning

*The survey takes approximately 15-60+ minutes of your time and may be forwarded to other team members for additional feedback and knowledge sharing.

If you would like to preview the survey or if you have any additional questions before taking the survey, please contact Tammy Smith with the Sapient Insights Research team at research@sapientinsights.com.       

The survey results will debut at the HR Technology Conference October 20-22nd 2026. Our team looks forward to seeing you there! If you would like to purchase a copy of our 2025-2026 Annual HR Systems Report, you can do so here.

Thank you for sharing your voice and supporting this amazing global research community!

Does anyone have any ideas if this is a possibility with he variable pay module?

entry.title: How to define "PEER" Rater Category/Role in SuccessFactors 360 Reviews
entry.content:
I am new to SuccessFactors 360 Reviews, and I am learning to create a new 360 Review form with the workflow/process as mentioned below.

The form should be supported by a route map that allows the Employee to specify the participants. This selection will subsequently be reviewed and approved by the employee's manager, after which the evaluation will be forwarded to multiple raters, including Direct Reports, Peers, and/or the Manager. Upon completion of the evaluation, the confidentiality of the participants' feedback must be preserved from the employee, although it may be accessible to the manager and administrators. Attached are snapshots of the roles that are currently displayed under the form and the route map.

1.) How can I define the role category of a "peer," which could encompass anyone within the organization but outside the employee's direct hierarchy? For instance, this could include an EMM's counterpart or a colleague from the operations team, among others. Additionally, once the nomination of participants is approved, how does the Evaluation step incorporate these nominations to facilitate the feedback request?

2.) How are external participants defined ?

3.) How is such a role delineated within the Evaluation step of the route map?

entry.title: SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionality
entry.content:
SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionality 

With the new release V3.0, SAP Mobile Start got rebranded as the Joule Work mobile app, with enhanced Joule integration and advanced single sign-on functionality. 

Rebranding: SAP Mobile Start has been rebranded as the Joule Work mobile app, accompanied by a new app icon and an updated user experience. For customers previously accessing Joule within SAP Mobile Start, the Joule entry point has transitioned from the diamond in the header bar to a full-screen interface that can be accessed via the tab bar. This provides users faster access to Joule.  

All core functionality and features remain unchanged, ensuring continuity for both customers and end users with no disruption to existing workflows.

 Screenshot: Direct Joule access in tab bar

For customers running the Joule Work mobile app within their SAP Build Work Zone environment, existing theming and custom branding capabilities remain unchanged. Customers can continue to apply their current branding configurations without impact.

Enhanced Joule capabilities: With the latest release of the app, Joule Work mobile allows users to dictate their prompts to Joule to allow a fast entry of prompts and a better hands-free experience.

Screenshot: Joule dictation

Single–sign-on:

Currently, users can enjoy a Single Sign‑On–like experience for web content by using the “Remind Me” functionality provided by SAP Cloud Identity Services. With the Joule Work mobile app version 3.0, the app offers an advanced Single Sign-On (SSO) capability, reducing the need for users to manually log in when navigating to trusted web applications in the in-app browser. We plan to release a dedicated blog post explaining the feature in depth shortly.

I hope you will enjoy these new features in the Joule Work mobile app.. 

SAP Mobile Experience offers intelligent native mobile solutions that help businesses build more efficient, resilient and sustainable end-to-end processes, improving people’s work life wherever they are.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Joule Work mobile app is here!
entry.content:
So, what is Joule Work? Joule Work provides an AI-based user experience that puts the user into the center; and transforms how users work. With Joule Work, users can express what they want to accomplish, and Joule surfaces the right insights, automates routine work, and coordinates AI agents across systems to achieve it – all in one dynamic workspace, on the desktop as well as on mobile. Joule Work acts as an engagement layer that spans across SAP products and beyond and coexists with current experiences provided by pre-defined application UIs. 

Our first version of the Joule Work mobile app comes with two main elements: Conversations and Space.
Let have a look the at the two.

Conversations are AI powered chats with role-based intents, conversation threads, and dynamic response types. Conversations are the main way to interact with the system, users can articulate their needs in a certain context, and Joule will take the required actions or provide the right answers.
The Joule Work mobile app also allows users to have hands-free voice conversations with Joule what is very helpful while being on the go. 

 

 

Screenshot: Conversations, Conversation with context and voice mode

Spaces are dynamic, intent-driven workspaces for more complex business problems. They are composed in real time to bring the right data from different systems, tools, and context together for a specific goal, freeing people from working in multiple applications and passive UIs.​ With the Joule Work mobile app, users can work with their spaces while being away from their desk.

Screenshot: Spaces

 

Please also check out our Joule Mobile sap.com page.  

Many of you might wonder, how does the new Joule Work mobile app relate to SAP Mobile Start. The Joule Work mobile app should be seen as an evolution of SAP Mobile Start. It will support both scenarios in the future.

It supports customers who want to explore the new Joule Work offering, as described in this blog with spaces and conversations. For customers running the Joule Work mobile app within their SAP Build Work Zone environment, all core functionality and features remain unchanged, ensuring continuity for both customers and end users with no disruption to existing workflows. Please also see my blog SAP Mobile Start - Rebranding of the app, enhanced Joule and improved single sign-on functionality.

I hope you will enjoy these features in the Joule Work mobile app.

Visit SAP Mobile Experience Community Page and click “follow” to get the latest development and innovation of our solutions. We look forward to hearing about your experience with setting up the solution in your landscape; please do share your thoughts and comments below. Enter here for additional questions regarding SAP Mobile Experience Applications.

Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: [Professional Services] SAP Learning Curriculum for Professional Services
entry.content:
SAP Learning Curriculum for Professional Services

The Professional Services industry is evolving rapidly – and so is the way we learn SAP solutions for project-based businesses.

To better support learners, SAP provides updated Professional Services learning journeys and courses, aligned with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and modern service delivery models.

This blog helps you understand the key learning offerings available today and how to navigate your learning journey with clarity and confidence.

Overview of Key Professional Services Courses

Below is an overview of the core Professional Services learning content, based on the latest offerings on learning.sap.com.

0. ISR010 – Introduction to Professional Services in SAP

Industry-level introduction and business context

  • Provides a high-level overview of the Professional Services industry and its key characteristics
  • Introduces how SAP supports project-based and service-driven business models
  • Helps learners understand the end-to-end value chain from bid to bill

Designed for:

  • Beginners exploring the industry
  • Learners needing business context before diving into S/4HANA
  • Non-technical roles

➡️Positioned as the recommended course to understand the basics of the industry

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-the-professional-services-industry

1. ISR20 – Exploring Fundamentals of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Entry-level introduction to Professional Services in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Focuses on SAP S/4HANA Cloud’s role in project delivery
  • Introduces key components such as:
    • Project management
    • Finance
    • Workforce management
  • Provides foundational understanding for working with SAP Professional Services solutions

Designed for:

  • Beginners
  • Consultants new to Professional Services

➡️Typically the starting point for the learning journey

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-fundamentals-of-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

2. ISR21 – Managing Projects and Resources in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Core course on project execution and resource management

  • Focuses on managing projects and resource allocation in Professional Services
  • Covers key activities such as:
    • Creating and managing resource requests
    • Understanding staffing processes
    • Matching resources to project requirements
  • Introduces time recording concepts, including:
    • Tracking work hours
    • Using SAP Fiori applications
    • Understanding role-based responsibilities

Designed for:

  • Project managers
  • Resource planners
  • Functional consultants

➡️Builds understanding of how to execute and manage projects effectively

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

3. ISR22 – Exploring Procurement Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Introduction to procurement processes supporting project delivery

  • Focuses on procurement of services and goods for project execution
  • Covers key topics such as:
    • Service procurement and goods purchasing
    • Third-party order processing
    • Supplier-related processes
  • Introduces core procurement elements including:
    • Requisitions
    • Supplier invoices
    • Contract creation

Designed for:

  • Consultants involved in sourcing and procurement
  • Project roles managing external resources or suppliers

➡️Explains how procurement processes integrate into Professional Services

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-procurement-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

4. ISR23 – Exploring Sales & Intercompany Processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Focus on sales, billing, and revenue processes

  • Covers end-to-end sales order processing in project-based scenarios
  • Includes key topics such as:
    • Sales orders and billing documents
    • Credit and debit memo management
    • Event-Based Revenue Recognition
  • Introduces intercompany processes for service delivery

Designed for:

  • Consultants working on billing and revenue processes
  • Project roles responsible for financial execution

➡️Provides understanding of how services are billed and revenue is recognized

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sales-intercompany-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

 

5. ISR30 – Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Professional Services

Implementation-focused course with hands-on scenarios

  • Focuses on implementing Professional Services solutions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Covers key areas such as:
    • HR connectivity
    • Project management
    • Sales order processing
    • Project billing
    • Intercompany processes
  • Includes practical learning elements:
    • Configuration lessons
    • System simulations
    • Hands-on exercises

Designed for:

  • Implementation consultants
  • Project team members preparing for real-world deployments

➡️Prepares learners for end-to-end implementation and practical project work

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services

 

Learning Journey – Exploring End‑to‑End Implementation & Operations SAP Cloud ERP, Professional Services 

Structured, role-based learning path

The main Learning Journey:

👉Exploring End-to-End Implementation & Operations SAP Cloud ERP, Professional Services

  • Guides learners through the complete Professional Services value chain
  • Covers:
    • Core fundamentals and project execution
    • Staffing and resource management
    • Time recording
    • Service procurement and supplier processes
    • Sales order processing and billing
    • Event-Based Revenue Recognition
  • Includes:
    • Real-world scenarios
    • Configuration lessons
    • Simulations and exercises
  • Leads to certification readiness

➡️Serves as the central, structured learning path for Professional Services

Learning.sap.com: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-end-to-end-implementation-operations-sap-cloud-erp-professional-services

What’s Changing in Professional Services Learning?

SAP learning for Professional Services has evolved to reflect:

  • Modern SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions
  • Strong focus on end-to-end business processes
  • Integration across:
    • Project delivery
    • Finance
    • Resource management

Key shift:

  • From isolated training topics
  • To structured learning journeys and role-based learning

Why These Courses Matter

The current curriculum helps you:

  • Understand how SAP supports project-based business models
  • Learn end-to-end processes instead of isolated features
  • Build skills aligned with:
    • Real project scenarios
    • Implementation and consulting roles

Professional Services solutions from SAP enable companies to:

  • Manage services more effectively
  • Optimize resources and workforce
  • Support XaaS-based business models
  • Analyze data across the service lifecycle

Recommended Learning Path

A typical progression:

  1. ISR010 – understand the basics of the industry
  2. ISR20 – Fundamentals. The starting point for the learning journey
  3. ISR21 – How to execute and manage projects effectively
  4. ISR22 – How procurement processes integrate into Professional Services
  5. ISR23 – How services are billed and revenue is recognized
  6. ISR30 – End-to-end implementation and practical project work
  7. Learning Journey – End-to-end structured learning

You Don’t Have to Do This Alone

To support your learning journey, the Industries Learning Group provides a dedicated place to:

  • Exchange with other learners
  • Ask questions:
    • Where should I start?
    • Which courses fit my role?
    • What should I learn next?
  • Share feedback on learning content

👉The goal is simple:
Help each other navigate SAP industry learning more easily

Get Started

If you are:

  • Starting your Professional Services learning journey
  • Preparing for SAP S/4HANA Cloud projects
  • Looking for structured enablement

👉Begin with ISR010, ISR20 or the Learning Journey
👉Follow the structured curriculum
👉Build your expertise step by step

Let’s learn SAP for Professional Services – together.

@Grzegorz

entry.title: Pro Rata VAT configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm currently working on Pro Rata VAT configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, and I've run into a limitation I'd like to get your input on.

As you may know, the standard "Define Pro Rata Coefficients" activity (under Accounting → Tax Management → Tax on Goods and Services → Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases). According to SAP documentation, only 23 Contries are supported.

My question is: what happens when your company code is based in a country that is NOT on this list?

From what I can see, the system does not allow you to define a Pro Rata coefficient for unsupported countries, which means the standard configuration path is blocked.

Have any of you faced this situation? Is there something I might be missing in the configuration, perhaps a workaround or an alternative approach to handle Pro Rata VAT calculations for countries outside the standard scope in Public Cloud?

entry.title: S/4HANA upgrade relevant report version check
entry.content:
Dear Support,

We have an upcoming project to migrate our system to S/4HANA.

In the future, we will execute and generate the following results:

  • Enable Digitally Signed SAP Notes
  • ATC Check
  • SAP Readiness Check
  • SI-Check (Simplification Item Check)

We would like to confirm whether, based on the current system version, it is possible to complete the above tasks only by applying SAP Notes, without performing any system version upgrade.

Please refer to the attached file for detailed version information.

Please support to check it. Thanks.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA upgrade relevant report version check
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

Greetings from SAP Readiness Check team.

As outlined in KBA 2913617 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA (and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition), it is technically possible to run the SAP Readiness Check on your current system version. However, we strongly recommend updating your software component version beforehand to avoid any potential inconsistencies, as your current version is significantly outdated.

For reference, please note the following guidance from the KBA:

While it is recommended to have the latest version of ST-PI implemented, it is possible to run SAP Readiness Check on older versions. If your system is on a lower ST-PI release (700 < SP17, 710 < SP17, or 740 < SP07), please deselect the "Data Volume Management" and "HANA Sizing Data" checkboxes when executing the check.

 

Thank you for you understanding.

Sincerely,

Yuki

Sincerely,

Yuki

entry.title: SAP Developer News Special Sapphire 2026 Edition
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Sapphire Innovation Guide

https://www.sap.com/topics/events/sapphire/innovation-news-guide-2026#introduction

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 Joule Studio

2:13 Integration Suite

3:44 ABAP

Transcription

00;00;00;07 - 00;00;06;21
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News Sapphire 2026 Special Edition.

00;00;06;23 - 00;00;09;26
Thomas Jung
All right. We're here at Sapphire in Orlando.

00;00;10;02 - 00;00;35;28
Thomas Jung
Actually, the keynote is going on right now, so it was one of the quietest times that we could record a news segment. But I wanted to share with you some of the news from the keynote in case you didn't catch it. There's a lot of developer related interesting news. First of all, the announcement of the new edition of Joule Studio, and I'm actually here in front of the Joule Studio Agent Lab, where people are going to be building agents with the new Joule Studio.

00;00;36;01 - 00;01;01;15
Thomas Jung
Rich, I and Josh are here to help out with that. But as described in the keynote, Joule Studio is a new tool for building agents and applications and extensions. But using an AI agent to do that. So it is Agentic development. You basically describe what you want to build and let the AI do a lot of the work.

00;01;01;15 - 00;01;20;07
Thomas Jung
It builds the specifications for you, you approve them, you iterate with the AI, and then it will start generating the code for you as well. But also discussing the keynote is our openness in this environment. We've integrated with other SAP capabilities like BDC.

00;01;20;10 - 00;01;52;09
Thomas Jung
LeanIX and many other SAP capabilities through MCP servers. And what the Joule studio creates is a code based agent approach. So everything that we're creating, you can push to GitHub and then open in a third party tool like Microsoft Visual Studio Code. We were doing that the other day in the agent lab. We created a project in Joule Studio, and then we opened it in VSCode and continued developing it with other coding agents like Claude.

00;01;52;14 - 00;02;12;01
Thomas Jung
And that's just one of the major announcements here at Sapphire. We're going to bring you in the news. Some of the other major announcements, like those around the upcoming release of ABAP for VS code, and an MSC server for ABAP and other developer related announcements later in the news segment, just for

00;02;12;04 - 00;02;18;12
Tushar Pradhan
Hi everyone. Good morning. This is Tushar, part of our integration product management team. And thank you for joining us at Sapphire.

00;02;18;14 - 00;02;41;04
Gayathri Narayana
Hi everyone. My name is Gayathri. I'm also from the Integration Suite product management team. I'm joining you live from SAP Sapphire in the Sunshine State. And we are very excited about two innovations I want to talk about. One is the MCP Gateway and Integration Suite, which enables agent developers to work with a curated set of APIs by cherry picking them and enabling a very governed and secured access.

00;02;41;05 - 00;02;51;28
Gayathri Narayana
The second functionality is Joule and Integration Suite, which enables our integration developers to be able to generate world class integrations, which covers message transformation and B2B scenarios.

00;02;52;00 - 00;03;14;18
Tushar Pradhan
Great great, great innovations. And let me talk about the innovation with the respect to the process orchestration agent that we have released. So this will allow you to find compatible, you know, reuse capabilities, but also able to give you generate Groovy scripts based on your existing PI/POas well as your custom adapters for your Java mapping. But that's not the end of it.

00;03;14;19 - 00;03;33;08
Tushar Pradhan
In Q4, we are also releasing an agent orchestration, which will help you, you know, just get your agent based integration, be able to generate based on that. And last but not least, the AI gateway, which is governed access to your AI, ensuring that your AI management is governed and is orchestrated well. But that's not the end of it.

00;03;33;09 - 00;03;37;29
Tushar Pradhan
Keep listening and keep hearing about Innovation Guide. You'll hear more about it from an integration soon.

00;03;38;07 - 00;03;44;19
Gayathri Narayana
Thank you. And don't forget to check check out the innovation guide which is linked in the description. Thank you so much and have.

00;03;44;21 - 00;03;46;03
Speaker 3
Bye bye.

00;03;46;05 - 00;04;07;15
Rich Heilman
Hey, friends. Rich Heilman here. We need to talk about ABAP in VS code. A major update is hitting us in June. We are officially releasing the ABAP MCP, and you might be wondering what an MCP actually is. It stands for Model Context Protocol. Just think of it as a secure bridge between your AI coding assistant and your SAP back in.

00;04;07;15 - 00;04;31;14
Rich Heilman
Right now, asking an AI to write ABAP It's like asking it to guess in the dark. It relies on generic web data. It has zero clue about your custom tables or your specific data dictionary. The MCP fixes that completely and that's your VSCode AI. Securely read your live about what does it mean for you? It means highly accurate code suggestions that actually compile.

00;04;31;17 - 00;04;49;20
Rich Heilman
The AI finally understands your specific system. You will spend a lot less time fixing weird AI hallucinations and much more time building solid code. We are really looking forward to getting this into your hands in June. I will have plenty of setup walkthroughs ready for you when it drops, but for now, I'll catch you later.

entry.title: Practice System: Fresh Start Option for Repeated Learner Sessions
entry.content:
Hi SAP Learning Community,

My name is Rejoice Jones, and I'm the Founder at Vower. We're currently evaluating the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud practice system for our learners and appreciate the information you've shared so far.

Question:

Once a learner has purchased access to the practice system, are they able to repeat the exercises  multiple times with a clean slate each session? Specifically, we'd like to confirm whether the system can be reset on demand (or reverts automatically) so that entries and data from previous practice sessions don't carry over.

This is important to us because repetition is a key part of how our learners build proficiency. Being able to work through each exercise from a fresh starting point, rather than continuing from where they left off, would allow them to reinforce the steps, identify gaps in their understanding, and gain confidence before applying the skills in a live environment.

Could you let me know if this is supported, and if so, how learners would initiate a reset?

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: How to use Migration Cockpit to extend vendor when the BP number is not the same as customer/vendor
entry.content:
1. Root Cause Analysis

The standard DMC migration object for vendor extensions is strictly designed under the assumption that the Supplier ID and the Business Partner (BP) ID are identical.

KBA 3410894 Error "Business partner XXXXXX doesn't exist" occurs when preparing or simulating the migration object

When processing the general structure data (S_SUPPL_GEN), the system executes a standard rule named BOR_LFA1_2 to validate and assign BP attributes (such as person/org names). In this rule, the system queries the BP master table (BUT000) using the Vendor Number (lv_lifnr). Because your system is configured to use different numbers for Vendors and BPs, the system searches for the Vendor ID inside the BP table, fails to find it, and triggers the hard error: "Business partner 0000200000 doesn't exist.”

2. Proposed Technical Solution

To bypass this standard limitation and enforce the use of different Vendor and BP numbers for existing vendor migrations, we need to customize the DMC object. I recommend the following approach:

  • Step 1: Structure Adjustment: Add an additional field to the source structure S_SUPPL_GEN in LTMOM (e.g., BP_NUMBER).
  • Step 2: Field Mapping: Map this new custom field to the target field BPARTNERHDRBP within the R_IT_DATA target structure.
  • Step 3: Rule Customization: Adjust the ABAP logic inside rule BOR_LFA1_2 so that the system searches BUT000 using the newly provided BP number, rather than defaulting to the Vendor number.

Technical Reference for your ABAP Team: 

Current Standard Logic:

Proposed Custom Logic:

DATA(lv_search_partner) = COND #( WHEN _wa_s_suppl_gen-bp_number IS NOT INITIAL 

                                   THEN _wa_s_suppl_gen-bp_number 

                                                 ELSE lv_lifnr ).

 

SELECT SINGLE name_first, 

              name_last, 

              name_org1, 

              name_org2, 

              type,

              partner_guid

  FROM but000

  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF ls_but000

  WHERE partner = lv_search_partner.

3. Important Disclaimers & Next Steps

Before we proceed with implementing this customization, please be aware of the following:

  • Iterative Testing Required: This custom logic will bypass the current validation error. However, the data will subsequently be passed to the core API (CMD_MIG_BP_CVI_CREATE). Because we are altering the standard data flow, we must extensively test this in a Development/Quality environment to ensure the core API accepts the split numbering without triggering secondary errors from technical and functional perspectives.
  • Support Impact (Crucial): Making these changes will convert the standard migration object into a "Customized Object." Consequently, it will no longer be covered by standard SAP Incident Support. The project team will need to take full responsibility for the maintenance and functional validation of this custom object moving forward.

Please review these findings with your technical and functional teams.

entry.title: MRP run considers substitute inventory
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA On-Premise edition. When running MRP, we expect the inventory of alternative materials to be included in the supply scope to cover the requirement quantity of the main material.
 
Example as below:
Product PRD_K has three components MAT_A, MAT_B and MAT_C in its BOM. The three materials are mutually alternative materials with priority 1, 2 and 3 respectively.
Inventory quantity of MAT_A: 50
Inventory quantity of MAT_B: 30
Inventory quantity of MAT_C: 20
We expect the following result during MRP run:
The requirement quantity of MAT_A is 120. The available stock of MAT_A cannot fully cover the demand. After consuming all stock of MAT_A (50), the system consumes the stock of alternative material MAT_B (30). If the demand is still not satisfied, it continues to consume the stock of alternative material MAT_C (20). Finally, there is still a shortage of 20 units. At this point, the system shall generate a purchase requisition of 20 units for MAT_A.
 
How should we configure standard SAP settings or implement enhancement development in SAP to realize this business scenario?

entry.title: SAP Business AI Platform: #1 Autonomous Enterprise - The Vision
entry.content:
"If you understand the vision...it's a strategy.

If you don't understand the vision, it's just a renaming."

At SAP Sapphire 2026 SAP introduced a big term that could redefine how businesses run with AI -  SAP Business AI Platform.

In this blog series, we will explore what SAP Business AI Platform is and why its so important in the world of AI and AI Agents.

This is the first blog, where we will focus on the the big question - Why SAP Business AI Platform is introduced. 
What is SAP's vision of Autonomous Enterprise and how SAP Business AI Platform would support that.

Disclaimer: This blog reflects my personal understanding, interpretation, and point of view. It is not an official publication or statement from SAP.

 

What is the Autonomous Enterprise?

 

Let’s imagine an organization where:

  • AI executes tasks automatically from start to finish.
  • AI detects issues before humans notice them.
  • AI makes decisions within approved boundaries.
  • And still humans stay in control.

That is exactly what we call the Autonomous Enterprise.

In simple words…

An Autonomous Enterprise is a business where:

  • AI assistants and AI agents manage and execute end to end processes,
  • At the same time people stay in control, focusing on judgment, strategy and the value creation that truly matters.

 

A Simple Real-Life Example

 

Humans Are Still in Charge and Important

This does not mean robots are taking over. It means only repetitive operational work is handled by AI.

So that people spend less time navigating systems and coordinating work, and more time focusing on strategic tasks and decisions that truly move the business.

The goal is not replacing people. The goal is amplifying people.

 

Autonomous Enterprise is NOT a New Product

Autonomous Enterprise is NOT a new product or solution.

Autonomous Enterprise is a Vision. It's an end-state for SAP customers to aspire to that only SAP can deliver.

 

5 Key Elements of the Autonomous Enterprise

 

 

1. Joule

Joule as the new engagement layer, bringing together the right data, workflow, and agents across SAP systems and beyond.

Joule will completely reinvent how end-users engage with SAP software and execute via assistants and agents.

 

2. SAP Autonomous Suite

SAP Autonomous Suite is the operational core of the business, organized into Autonomous Domains where people set the direction and assistants and agents execute the work.

The Autonomous Suite is comprised of five autonomous domains:

  1. Autonomous Finance
  2. Autonomous Spend
  3. Autonomous Supply Chain Management (SCM)
  4. Autonomous HCM
  5. Autonomous CX.

Each autonomous domain is comprised of specialized assistants and agents

 

3. Industry AI

Many customers’ most complex and valuable business challenges are highly industry specific. Industry AI extends the autonomous domain model with vertical depth, embedding sector-specific process knowledge, data models, and regulatory logic.  

 

4. SAP Business AI platform

SAP Business AI platform as the new foundation bringing together:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)
  • parts of SAP Business Transformation Management (BTM)
  • and AI Foundation

to deliver business context, unified data, models, and enterprise-grade governance

In the next blog, we will discuss more on what exactly SAP Business AI platform is.

 

5. Accelerate Adoption

For most customers, migration and implementation costs remain the primary barrier to adopting our cloud solutions and moving away from legacy or 3rd party solutions.

To address this, SAP is investing significantly in AI‑assisted business transformation, augmenting the RISE with SAP journey with migration and modernization assistants that fundamentally improve the efficiency of ERP transformation.

Powered by agent-led toolchain and backed by the RISE with SAP Methodology, this approach reduces effort and time, making the path to the cloud more efficient.

For new cloud adopters, SAP GROW pairs pre-configured best practices with AI-assisted implementation for faster time to value.

 

Why Autonomous Enterprise is important?

Most companies are using AI in isolated pockets… a chatbot here, an automation there. But isolated use cases deliver only incremental value.

The real transformation happens when AI is embedded directly into end-to-end business processes, working with trusted business data and enterprise governance.

That is SAP’s vision of the Autonomous Enterprise… where AI does not just answer questions, but actively helps run the business.

Not AI for demos. AI for outcomes. 

Not AI as a feature. AI as the operating model for the future enterprise.


Hope this helped you understand the Autonomous Enterprise. Got any queries, let me know in comment!

Next Blog:

SAP Business AI Platform: #2 What Exactly It is and It's Core Components [To be published tomorrow]

entry.title: Re: ONB Custom MDF data entered by HR not visible in Onbo...
entry.content:
Hello @LukaFeige ,

Here are a few reasons why the external user may not be able to view the data entered by the hiring manager in NHDR:

  1. The external user visibility is not set to the object. The external user visibility should be set via a business rule. This will allow the system to read the values stored in manage data for an external user.
  2. The permissions to the custom MDF Object should be granted via the OnboardingExternalUser role. 
  3. The MDF Object should not be effective dated. In onboarding, we only support MDF Objects if the effective dating is set to "None".
  4. externalCode associated with the custom MDF Object should be "User"
  5. The Hire Template associated in PDC step should include the custom UI object from the second portlet (avoid adding the custom MDF from within the first portlet such as Identity portlet).

Regards,

Akarsh

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA upgrade relevant report version check
entry.content:
Hello,


Yes, these activities may be possible by applying SAP Notes only, but it depends on your current system component versions.


Digitally Signed SAP Notes
Requires specific SNOTE / SAP_BASIS / Kernel prerequisites. If your system is too old, only SAP Notes may not be enough.


ATC Check
Can usually be enabled by SAP Notes, but required SAP_BASIS and ST-PI/ST-A/PI versions must be available.


SAP Readiness Check
Requires implementing SAP Notes and running data collectors. Some minimum support package levels may be required.


SI-Check / Simplification Item Check
Usually delivered via SAP Notes, but also depends on system release and support package level.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: How to add a check for requisitioner for purchase req...
entry.content:
Hello @Aavil 


Use enhancement/BAdI at PM order save before database update, and raise an error message to stop save/PR creation and return to IW31/IW32.
Recommended enhancements to check


BAdI WORKORDER_UPDATE

Method: AT_SAVE
Use it to stop order save with error message.
But if operation/component data is missing, read current order buffer using standard PM order APIs/function modules.

 

User exit IWO10009

Enhancement: IWO10009
Purpose: Check PM order before save
This is commonly used for validation during IW31/IW32 save.

 

BAdI ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST

If PR is created from PM order, validate requisitioner at PR item level.
Method: PROCESS_ITEM / CHECK
Raise error to stop PR creation.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: JSON Payload Download Implementation in RAP Application
entry.content:
Hello @Yash_Rudraksha 


In RAP, don’t download JSON directly from ABAP action.
Instead, return the JSON string from RAP action and trigger download in the Fiori/UI5 side.
RAP action output
Create action with result:
action DownloadJson result [1] $self;


RAP action prepares JSON,
UI5 downloads JSON file,
ABAP RAP action cannot directly open browser download dialog
Same payload can be reused for API call and download


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: SCU3
entry.content:
Hello @Gajey 


Yes, you can create a Z ALV report, but don’t read DBTABLOG-LOGDATA directly. It is stored as LRAW/encoded table record data, so direct SELECT will not give old/new values properly. Use SAP standard logic/FM instead. DBTABLOG stores table change logs and LOGDATA is the modified data record in LRAW format.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: How I can control MOQ in a sales order not by SKU but...
entry.content:
Your requirement is not clear and need more details.  Answer the below questions
 
1. Scope of MOQ Control
Should MOQ apply to the entire order total (all SKUs combined) or to each SKU line item?  Do you want MOQ rules to apply universally or only for specific customers, regions, or order types?
 
2. Business Logic
If an order contains multiple SKUs, should MOQ be enforced on the combined total quantity or individually per SKU?  What should happen if MOQ is not met?
  • Block the order submission
  • Show a warning message
  • Auto-adjust quantities to meet MOQ
 
3. Flexibility & Exceptions
Should MOQ rules be configurable by product category, customer, or sales channel?  Do you need exceptions (e.g., promotional orders, samples, special customers)?
 
4. Operational Impact
How should MOQ interact with other rules like pricing tiers, discounts, or shipping constraints? At what stage should MOQ be validated?
  • During order entry
  • At approval stage
  • At fulfillment
5. Example Scenario
Please provide a concrete example of an order where MOQ should apply (e.g., “Any order must have at least 100 units total, regardless of SKU mix”).

entry.title: Re: How to use BAPE_SDSALESDOCITEM to update custom VBAP...
entry.content:
Hi @bigflatfoot ,
Are you using Public Cloud Edition?

How do you call your coding? Are you using events? 

In general you could also use a Custom Application Job and update Sales Orders from Reference Documents via release RAP BO Interface I_SalesOrderTP. You can find an example here. This job could select the changed reference documents every day / hour / minute via release CDS Views I_SalesContract.

If you want to have the update in real time, you could also use local event consumption and update Sales Orders immediately when the contract or quotation has been changed. You can still use the Application Job as a backup if the update from the local event handler fails (for example because the sales order is currently locked).

entry.title: How to: Mass Update of Material Group and Purchasing Group in the Material Master (Public Cloud)
entry.content:
This post describes a simple and repeatable approach to mass‑updating the Material Group and Purchasing Group in the material master using standard apps in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

 

The Mass Update Process consists of three main steps:

Step 1: Export Master Data – Products

Fiori App: Export Master Data – Products (F8324)

  • Select the required fields:
    • Material Group (MARA‑MATKL)
    • Purchasing Group (MARC‑EKGRP)
  • Include all material numbers that need to be changed
  • Export the data using “Export” to download the Excel file

Step 2: Maintain Data in Excel

  • Update the Material Group and Purchasing Group values in the Excel file
  • Side Note:
    • Material Group is defined on client‑level (plant‑independent)
    • Purchasing Group is define plant‑specific and must be maintained for each relevant plant

Step 3: Manage Mass Maintenance – Products

Fiori App: Manage Mass Maintenance – Products (F8326)

  • Start a new mass maintenance process
  • Upload the updated Excel file

 

  • SAP automatically determines the fields in scope
  • Start the process via “Start”

 

 

  • In status “Stopped for Review”, review the proposed changes
  • Confirm execution by selecting “Continue” twice

 

 

 

  • Once the process reaches “Completed”, all changes are applied and can be verified in the product master

 

Summary

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, mass changes to Material Group and Purchasing Group in the material master can be handled efficiently by using a three step approach. SAP supports the process with validation steps throughout the process chain. 

References

  • Export Master Data | SAP Help Portal
  • Manage Mass Maintenance | SAP Help Portal
  • Start Mass Maintenance | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: What the announcements really mean for ECC and S/4HANA customers
entry.content:
The five key building blocks

1. Autonomous Enterprise: Humans set the direction, AI executes.

The Autonomous Enterprise is the overarching concept behind everything SAP is announcing this year. The idea: decisions are based on real-time data, workflows run end-to-end, and AI continuously improves operational processes. Crucially for regulated industries, SAP emphasizes fully governed AI – meaning AI that is transparent and controlled. Especially when it comes to financial, operational, and regulatory decisions, this isn't just a marketing ploy, but a prerequisite for using these functions productively at all.

2. Joule Work: One interface for the entire company

Joule Work combines AI assistants and agents in a single workspace. Instead of switching between applications, users define what they want to achieve – the system then coordinates the steps, gathers information, and initiates workflows.

From a user's perspective, this is probably the most tangible change: less "where can I find that?" and more "what do I want to do?". Whether this actually reduces friction in everyday use depends on how well the agents are integrated into the respective business processes – and on which SAP edition the customer is running (more on this below).

3. SAP Autonomous Suite: The operational core

Across finance, supply chain, procurement, HR, and customer experience, assistants and agents collaborate to manage entire processes—not just individual tasks. The shift from "automating individual steps" to "end-to-end execution" is conceptually crucial.

In practice, this shifts routine work. Employees focus on decisions and exceptions instead of manually coordinating workflows.

4. Industry AI: Solutions that understand the industry context

Generic AI functions are often insufficient in regulated or highly specialized industries. With Industry AI , SAP combines applications, data, and AI building blocks to meet the specific requirements of individual industries.

For customers in pharmaceuticals, automotive, banking, utilities or the public sector, this is likely to be more relevant than broad cross-functional areas – provided that the respective industry solution is actually available and not just announced.

5. SAP Business AI Platform: The basis

The platform brings together business data, applications, and AI services in a single environment. It addresses a problem that most companies are familiar with from their own pilot projects: Getting a single AI use case up and running is feasible; operating it consistently, securely, and scalably is significantly more difficult. The platform is the prerequisite for transforming individual use cases into a viable operating model.

The honest question is: How much of this actually reaches ECC customers?

One must be realistic here. A large part of the announced AI functions – especially Joule Work, the end-to-end agent orchestration of the Autonomous Suite, and many Industry AI building blocks – are based on the modern SAP platform, usually S/4HANA Cloud (Public or Private Edition).

For ECC customers, this means specifically:

  • Mainstream maintenance for ECC 6.0 ends at the end of 2027; extended maintenance is available for an additional fee until the end of 2030.
  • The more ambitious AI promises cannot be implemented on ECC, or only to a very limited extent.
  • The Sapphire announcements increase the pressure to migrate to S/4HANA – but they do not change the known timeline.

This is no surprise, but rather the logical continuation of SAP's established strategy. Those still running on ECC should view these announcements less as "new features for me" and more as an additional reason to finalize their migration plans.

And what about S/4HANA customers?

Here too, a distinction is worthwhile, because not all S/4HANA is the same:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition will receive the new AI features the fastest and most completely.
  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition / RISE with SAP receives a large portion of the functions, depending on the release level and the chosen service model.
  • S/4HANA On-Premise traditionally has the least and slowest access to new AI features. Those who remain with this system should realistically assess which of Sapphire's promises will actually be relevant – and which will not.

For many customers, this reignites the debate about the right S/4 edition. The question of "cloud or on-premise?" increasingly determines the pace of innovation one can achieve.

Perhaps the most practical part: Agent-led Transformation

Perhaps the most important announcement from a customer perspective comes almost as an aside: SAP is using AI not only in operations, but also in the transformation itself. Joule Agents and Assistants are intended to automate tasks such as system analysis, data preparation, and test support.

This is particularly relevant for ECC customers facing an S/4 migration. Migrations rarely fail due to technical issues alone, but rather due to the effort required for analysis, cleansing, customization evaluation, and testing. If agent-led transformation measurably reduces these effort items, it's a practical lever – and not just a marketing ploy.

The important thing remains: Each customer should assess for their specific situation how much of this is already productively usable today and how much is merely a roadmap. A proof of concept on a real subsystem is more informative than any slideshow.

Conclusion

SAP Sapphire 2026 doesn't mark a break, but rather a consolidation of the strategy of recent years: AI becomes an operational component of the suite, not an optional add-on. The direction is clear, and the benefits for customers on modern editions are plausible.

For the majority of existing SAP customers – with ECC or older S/4 releases – the most important message is less technical than planning-related: The gap between "I'll stay on my current environment" and "I can use the new features" is widening. Agent-led transformation is intended to close this gap; whether it succeeds in practice will become clear in concrete projects over the coming months.

Anyone looking for a complete overview of all announcements can find it in the SAP Sapphire Innovation News Guide 2026 .

entry.title: Re: How to give logic for triggering a mail to quality ta...
entry.content:
Logic for Picking Mail IDs

1) Identify the Task Processor Type

  • If individual → pick directly from user master data (e.g., SAP HR infotype with email).
  • If department → resolve to a distribution list mapped to that department + plant.

2) Mapping Department to Distribution List
Create an Organizational Unit (OU) in PPOME (e.g., “Quality Team”). Assign departments under this OU. For each department, maintain a mapping table (custom Z-table or config) linking:

  • Plant
  • Department
  • Distribution List ID

3) Distribution List Setup
Use SO23 to create distribution lists (e.g., DL_QUALITY_1000). Add all relevant mail IDs for that plant’s department. Ensure naming convention is consistent (e.g., DL_<Dept>_<Plant>).

4) Logic in Notification Release

  • When task is released:
    • Check if processor = department.
    • Read plant from notification header.
    • Lookup mapping table for (Plant + Department).
    • Fetch distribution list ID.
    • Trigger mail to distribution list via SAPconnect/Outlook integration.

entry.title: Upgrading your SAPUI5 Rich Text Editor: Beyond TinyMCE version 4
entry.content:
Rich Text Editor in SAPUI5

The Rich Text Editor is an SAPUI5 control for data input and editing. It is used to enter rich text with different styles, colors, and formatting. The Rich Text Editor allows users to format text and add different types of elements, such as images and hyperlinks.

Screenshot of the SAPUI5 Rich Text Editor control

See details on

  • sap.ui.richtexteditor.RichTextEditor: Design, documentation, API reference and samples
  • RichTextEditor building block: Documentation and documentation in SAP Fiori Development Portal

Dependency on TinyMCE

For its implementation, this control uses the third-party component TinyMCE (see https://www.tiny.cloud/). When Tiny deprecates a version of TinyMCE, this version might still be in use in some versions of SAPUI5. Should issues arise, the SAPUI5 team tries to fix them by working around gaps. However, sometimes this is not possible, and updating TinyMCE is necessary, even in maintenance versions of SAPUI5. This is especially important for security, an area where SAPUI5 must address upcoming risks for all customers.

This dependency adds restrictions to the usage and support of the Rich Text Editor control, see the documentation. If your app uses a deprecated version of TinyMCE, the console shows a respective error message stating that this version of TinyMCE is deprecated and will be removed in future.

What happens in July 2026?

 

SAPUI5 will stop loading the deprecated TinyMCE version 4 by default.
Instead, it will load the latest available version.

This will happen in the following SAPUI5 versions:

  • SAPUI5 1.71   - TinyMCE deprecated as of 1.71.62
  • SAPUI5 1.84   - TinyMCE deprecated as of 1.84.42
  • SAPUI5 1.96   - TinyMCE deprecated as of 1.96.28
  • SAPUI5 1.108 - TinyMCE deprecated as of 1.108.0 

What does this mean for you?

As always, you benefit from improved features and qualities of the SAPUI5 Rich Text Editor. If, and only if, your app relies on TinyMCE version 4, the app will break until you use the EditorType property to explicitly stay with version 4. SAP does not recommend using deprecated software, as explained above.

What is planned for 2026?

The SAPUI5 team further plans to permanently remove TinyMCE version 4 from the SAPUI5 code later in 2026. This means there will be no way to load it anymore, no will workaround available. SAP strongly recommends removing any dependency to TinyMCE version 4.

Call to Action

Avoid any dependency on a specific (deprecated) version of TinyMCE to always benefit from the latest features and qualities in your SAPUI5 apps.

entry.title: ABAP AI - Chapter 3: We go agentic!
entry.content:
Summary

  • SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in all releases as of 2021 with planned start in Q2/2026
  • SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities will be priced on a consumption basis via AI Units
  • ABAP Development Tools for VS Code are planned to be GA in Q2/2026
  • An ABAP MCP Server will be shipped in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse and ABAP Development Tools for VS Code as of Q2/2026 enabling agentic ABAP development experience
  • An ABAP Custom Code Transformation agent focusing on the migration from SAP ECC towards SAP S/4HANA is planned for Q2/2026

Intro

Since the start of ABAP AI in early 2025 (“Joule speaks ABAP!”), the update in September 2025 (“ABAP AI – Chapter 2”), now it is time to deliver on the promises made at SAP TechEd 2025 (“Introducing the next era of ABAP development”) – time for chapter 3!

We are entering the agentic era of ABAP development: let's get started.

SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities in lower releases

As of June 2026, Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities are offered through a running on SAP BTP. This hub service combines authentication, license check, access to SAP BTP AI Core and use case specific business logic and can be plugged directly to the developer’s IDE. This way, we can offer ABAP AI capabilities mostly independent of the ABAP backend release. This enables us to serve SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition on releases 2021 and higher with Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities.

  • Planned availability in Q2/26
  • Supported releases: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2021 and higher
  • Costs: See below
  • Configuration guide and further information will follow and will be linked here

New commercial approach for ABAP AI

The free promotion for J4D ABAP AI capabilities comes to an end in September 2026. Accordingly, it is now time to launch the commercial model: Based on AI units, ABAP AI capabilities are now priced purely based on consumption. This approach combines transparency regarding the real costs based on the usage and is well-suited for dynamic and individual agentic AI use cases.

  • Planned availability in Q2/26
  • Materials: 8019164 (customers) and 8019670 (partners)
  • Information on how to purchase and activate will follow soon and will be linked here

ABAP Development Tools for VS Code

14 years after the launch of ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, time has come to support a new IDE. As of May 29th 2026, ABAP Development Tools will be available for VS Code. Building on the ABAP Language Server (see “Behind the Design…”), coupled with an ABAP MCP Server, this is the enabler for a truly agentic ABAP development experience, boosting development efficiency to the next level. It sets the foundation to leverage modern coding agents like GitHub Copilot directly for ABAP development. While we start with a small set of development objects and IDE tools focusing on SAP Fiori App development based on the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), we will very soon enhance the scope of object types to serve high priority development scenarios.

The VS Code extension is

  • planned to be available as of Q2/26
  • Supported releases: release 7.31 and higher
  • Works in the Cloud and OnPrem
  • Costs: free of charge
  • Precise info on how to obtain and get started will follow soon and will be linked here

MCP Support for ABAP development

The Language Server provides the required abstraction to gain independence of the IDE sitting on top. A major component of this delivery is the MCP server for ABAP development. It provides powerful tools which help coding agents to perform complex ABAP coding tasks. While the Language Server is currently delivered as part of the ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse and for VS Code, a potential standalone-shipment of the Language and MCP Server is under discussion.

  • Release planned for Q2/26
  • Supported in Eclipse and VS Code
  • Works in the Cloud and OnPrem
  • Supported for release 7.31 and higher
  • Precise info on how to use it in the IDE will follow soon and will be linked here

Agentic custom code migration for the SAP S/4HANA transformation

Bringing all pieces together, we are launching the first ABAP custom code agent assisting in the transformation of custom code from ECC towards SAP S/4HANA. The required capabilities are offered through the ABAP Language and MCP Server explained above and can be used leveraging a coding agent, for example Amazon Q or GitHub Copilot. Currently, Eclipse must be used for this agent, until all required ABAP object types are supported in VS Code (see above). This agent boosts our customers on the RISE journey when migrating their classic landscape to the Cloud ERP.

  • Availability planned for Q2/26.
  • Supported in Eclipse
  • Prerequisites: Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities via the new commercial approach with AI units (see above) and coding agent on top (e.g. Amazon Q or Github Copilot)
  • Further information to follow soon and will be linked here

 

Finally - please have a look at our FAQ document for frequent questions and watch out for the GA releases of these deliverables. We will keep you posted.

Get yourself ready to dive into agentic ABAP development! 😎

entry.title: Re: Best Practices Recommendations for SAP Datasphere
entry.content: I fully agree with separating authorisations into a single space, and one suggestion SAP should take into account is that, in the intelligent apps it is proposing to customers, there is a Central Permissions table that is used in the ingestion space rather than being placed in a separate space. Why am I mentioning this? Because when you want to customise the harmonisation and analytics spaces, you have to make a copy of them, and in the copy, you may not want to have the same level of authorisations as you have in the standard intelligent app, so authorisation maintenance is duplicated. And if, on top of that, you want a separate space to build all your customisation and keep it separate from the copy, you have to set up another level of authorisations in that space, meaning maintenance is tripled. What would be the solution? In SAP Intelligent Applications, generate the DACS and the Central Permissions table in a separate authorisation space, so that these authorisations can be shared across the different usage spaces. Wouldn’t that be more optimal?

entry.title: Revenue Recognition Customer Projects - Credit Memo
entry.content:
Hi, 

Our customer is running Professional service projects, mainly Customer Project with Periodic Service scenario in SAP Cloud ERP. 

During the month of April they issued credit invoices in a specific project, when running the revenue recognition in the closing they found out that the Revenue Recognition doesn't take the credit memos into consideration and to make sure a correct result they created a manual booking. When searching for this I found KBA 3201424, which is exactly the problem they have identified. Based on the KBA we understand that when the project is closed this will be cleared, however when having projects for a long time this will mean more manual work. We'll need to understand how the customer should behave when they need to create credit invoices for projects with periodic services to avoid manual work? 

Kind Regards, Sanne
